title: Job Evaluation
description: Paying the wrong salaries costs you twice — you either lose good people to competitors or waste money overpaying the wrong roles. Job evaluation and salary benchmarking give you a fair, defensible pay structure that retains staff, controls costs, and protects you from equal pay disputes at the CCMA. OptiHR uses the Paterson Job Grading System — South Africa's most widely used and CCMA-recognised evaluation methodology — to grade every role objectively and benchmark salaries against current market data.

whyOptiHR.title: Paterson job grading
whyOptiHR.subtitle: South Africa's most trusted method
whyOptiHR.body: We analyse each role, write clear job descriptions signed by both employee and manager, and grade every position using the Paterson Job Grading System from the bottom up — ensuring internal fairness across your entire organisation. The Paterson System is recognised by unions, government, and the CCMA, making your pay structure legally defensible.

whyOptiHR.title: South African salary benchmarking
whyOptiHR.subtitle: current market data, your industry
whyOptiHR.body: We compare your salaries against current South African market data for similar roles in your industry and region. You'll know exactly where you're overpaying, underpaying, or positioned correctly — with a clear roadmap showing which salaries to adjust and when.

whyOptiHR.title: Equal pay compliance & CCMA protection
whyOptiHR.subtitle: defensible, fair, and documented
whyOptiHR.body: The Employment Equity Amendment Act requires equal pay for work of equal value. Without a formal grading structure, equal pay claims are extremely difficult to defend. Our job evaluation process creates the documented rationale required to prove your pay decisions are based on objective job content — not favouritism or discrimination.

legalConsiderations.title: Employment Equity Act — Equal Pay
legalConsiderations.subtitle: equal pay for work of equal value.
legalConsiderations.body: The EEA Amendment Act requires employers to eliminate unjustifiable pay differentials between employees performing work of equal value. Without a formal job grading structure, you cannot demonstrate your pay decisions are defensible — leaving you exposed to CCMA equal pay disputes.

legalConsiderations.title: Labour Relations Act
legalConsiderations.subtitle: pay disputes and unfair labour practices.
legalConsiderations.body: Pay-related disputes — including equal pay claims and remuneration disputes — can be referred to the CCMA as unfair labour practices. A documented, objective job evaluation structure is your primary defence against such claims.

faq.question: What is the Paterson Job Grading System?
faq.answer: The Paterson System grades jobs based on the complexity of decision-making required — from routine operational decisions at the lowest grades to strategic policy decisions at the highest. It is South Africa's most widely used job evaluation methodology, recognised by the CCMA, trade unions, and government. It provides an objective, internally consistent hierarchy that is difficult to challenge.

faq.question: Do we need job evaluation even if we're a small business?
faq.answer: Yes, if you have more than a handful of employees. Without a pay structure, salaries are set by negotiation — which almost always results in employees doing the same work earning different amounts. This creates resentment, high turnover, and potential equal pay claims. A simple, clear structure prevents all of these problems, regardless of company size.

faq.question: What happens if employees exaggerate their job content to get a higher grade?
faq.answer: Job inflation is common — employees often describe their roles as more complex than they actually are when grades are linked to pay. OptiHR uses objective job analysis, cross-referencing role descriptions against actual duties, organisational structure, and the reporting relationships of comparable roles. We identify inflated descriptions and keep grading objective.

faq.question: How does job evaluation protect us from CCMA equal pay claims?
faq.answer: An equal pay claim requires the claimant to show that they perform work of equal value to a comparator earning more, based on criteria that constitute unfair discrimination. A documented Paterson grading structure demonstrates that pay differences are based on objective job content differences — not race, gender, or any other discriminatory ground — which is the most effective defence available.

faqBody: Questions we hear from business owners and HR managers about pay structures and job grading in South Africa.

sections.text: Job evaluation grades every role in your organization based on what the job requires—not who's doing it or how well they negotiate . Salary benchmarking compares your pay to the South African market so you know if you're competitive . Together, they create a pay structure you can defend, afford, and trust .
What we do:
Job Analysis — We study each job to understand what it really involves: tasks, decisions, responsibilities, working conditions, and the skills needed .

Job Descriptions — We write clear, detailed job descriptions that define duties, authority, and reporting lines. Every job description is signed off by the employee and manager to prevent disputes later .

Job Grading — We use the Paterson Job Grading System (South Africa's most widely used method) to grade jobs into a fair hierarchy based on decision-making complexity . Jobs are graded from the bottom up, ensuring internal fairness across your entire organization .

Salary Benchmarking — We compare your salaries to current market data for similar roles in your industry and region across South Africa . You'll know exactly where you're overpaying, underpaying, or spot-on .
What you get:
Clear job descriptions for every role
A fair job grading structure with documented rationales for every grade
Market salary ranges for each grade
A roadmap showing which salaries to adjust and when
Protection from equal pay claims and CCMA disputes
Better retention—staff stay when they're paid fairly
C. Who Is This For?
Businesses with no formal pay structure who set salaries based on gut feel or negotiation
Companies with staff complaining about unfair pay or favouritism
Growing businesses in Gauteng and across South Africa adding new roles and unsure what to pay
Employers preparing for Employment Equity audits needing to prove equal pay for equal work
Organizations with high staff turnover where people leave for better pay
Private schools needing fair salary structures for educators and support staff
Companies facing equal pay claims or CCMA remuneration disputes
Businesses preparing for mergers, acquisitions, or investor due diligence
Legal expertise built in — Our owner is an admitted attorney who understands South Africa's equal pay laws. Your grading system will be CCMA-proof .
We use the Paterson System — South Africa's most trusted job evaluation method, recognized by unions, government, and the CCMA .
We do the work, not just advise — We write job descriptions, chair grading committees, benchmark salaries, and help you implement changes .
We prevent job inflation — Employees sometimes exaggerate their roles to get higher grades. We identify inflated job descriptions and keep grading objective .
Market data you can trust — We use current South African salary data from reputable sources and can arrange specialist salary surveys for your industry .
